title: Degenerate cases: $\omega=0$, collapsed intervals, low degree, defective eigenvalues

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
The four-vertex criterion and its proof steps must be checked at boundaries where they can fail or where fewer than four distinct vertices exist:
- $\omega_0=0$: a real eigenvalue at the origin corresponds to $a_0=0$; if $0\in[a_0^-,a_0^+]$ the family contains a non-Hurwitz member regardless of vertices — must be handled in the boundary-crossing and monotonicity lemmas.
- Collapsed coefficient intervals $a_i^-=a_i^+$: fewer than $2^n$ distinct vertices; the four Kharitonov matrices may coincide.
- Low degree $n=1,2$: fewer than four distinct Kharitonov vertices; verify the criterion reduces correctly.
- Defective / repeated imaginary eigenvalues: the eigenvector $v_\omega$ argument assumes a genuine eigenvector; address Jordan-block cases via the resolvent/limit.

**To prove:** each degenerate case explicitly, confirming the $\iff$ holds (or precisely stating the boundary convention) and that no step in the main proof silently assumed $n\ge3$, distinct vertices, or simple eigenvalues.
